title: crataegus oxyacantha var. granatensis
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha var. granatensis, also known as the hawthorn tree, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is a variant of the Crataegus oxyacantha species, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to support cardiovascular health. The leaves and berries of the Crataegus oxyacantha var. granatensis contain flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins, and other compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, improving heart function, and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, which can be used to promote cardiovascular health and well-being.
